prevalence.source: Eating disorder epidemiology syntheses (Latin America) / WHO context

burden.mortalityRate: Elevated vs general population (indirect; comorbidity and suicide risk)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: recurrent binge eating with compensatory behaviors (vomiting, laxatives, excessive exercise) at least weekly for 3 months plus body image overvaluation; assess electrolyte risk

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often detected in mental health services or after medical complications (electrolytes, dental issues)
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: CBT as first-line, nutritional counseling, SSRIs (fluoxetine) when indicated; medical monitoring for purging complications
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited specialized programs and delayed help-seeking
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Core antidepressants available in SUS; specialist services exist in major urban centers
